criteria_results.description: On screens narrower than 768px (MOBILE_BREAKPOINT), home.tsx renders only Canvas and MobileBottomBar — Toolbar, LayerPanel, ObjectToolbar, and PropertiesPanel are not rendered.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/pages/home.tsx (lines 25-33), when isMobile is true, it renders a layout containing only <Canvas /> and <MobileBottomBar />. The desktop components (Toolbar, ObjectToolbar, LayerPanel, PropertiesPanel) are only rendered in the else branch.

criteria_results.description: MobileBottomBar.tsx exports a default or named React component that reads selectedObjectIds from useUI() and switches between 'list' and 'edit' mode via useEffect.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx (lines 40-46), a useEffect dependent on uiState.selectedObjectIds sets mode to 'edit' if length > 0, otherwise 'list'.

criteria_results.description: In list mode, MobileBottomBar renders add-icon buttons for at least 5 icon types (circle, camera, square, triangle, hexagon) each calling handleAddIcon(iconType) from useObjectCreation.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx (lines 87-111), there are 6 buttons for 'circle', 'camera', 'square', 'triangle', 'hexagon', and 'arrow-right', each calling handleAddIcon with the respective type.

criteria_results.description: In list mode, MobileBottomBar renders a scrollable list of docState.objects where each item dispatches SELECT_OBJECT(obj.id) on click.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx (lines 124-148), docState.objects.map is used to render the list, and line 136 contains the onClick dispatch for SELECT_OBJECT.

criteria_results.description: In edit mode, MobileBottomBar renders an Input bound to firstObject.name that dispatches UPDATE_OBJECTS on change, and renders ObjectPhotoGallery with the selected object's id and photos.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx (lines 158-166), an Input is bound to selectedObject.name with UPDATE_OBJECTS dispatch. Lines 171-174 render ObjectPhotoGallery with objectId and photos props.

criteria_results.description: MobileBottomBar has a hide/show toggle: when hidden, only a floating pill button is visible; when visible, panel has fixed bottom-0 positioning with h-[50vh] and a CSS transition.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx (lines 52-61), a floating Button with ChevronUp is returned if !isBarVisible. Lines 65-81 show the panel with 'fixed bottom-0', 'h-[50vh]', and a transition-transform class.

criteria_results.description: Edit mode back button dispatches SELECT_OBJECT with null (or empty array via SET_SELECTION) to deselect, returning to list mode.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx (lines 48-50), handleBackToList dispatches SELECT_OBJECT with null. This function is called by the back button on line 70.

criteria_results.description: ObjectPhotoGallery file input has capture="environment" attribute for native mobile camera access.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/ObjectPhotoGallery.tsx (line 110), the input element has { ...({ capture: 'environment' } as any) } applied.

suggestions: In ObjectPhotoGallery.tsx, the 'multiple' attribute on the file input might conflict with 'capture="environment"' on some mobile browsers (often ignoring capture if multiple is present). Consider removing 'multiple' if you want to force direct camera access on mobile.

The transition-transform class is present on MobileBottomBar, but no actual transform style (like translate-y-full when hidden) is applied to the visible panel div; instead, it's conditionally rendered. For a smooth animation, you might want to keep it rendered but transform it off-screen.
